trajectory.description: Observation: Starting the experiment. Analysis: We need a baseline model to measure iteration time and establish a baseline score. I will use the pre-downloaded ESM-2 35M model, freeze its weights, and train a simple linear regressor on top of the CLS token representation. I will train for 2 epochs and submit for evaluation immediately. Decision: Write model.py, train.py, and predict.py, then run train.py and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: The baseline model achieved a spearman_rho of 0.3051. The iteration took ~6 minutes for 2 epochs with frozen weights. Analysis: The model is learning, but we can improve it by unfreezing the last few layers of the ESM model (fine-tuning) and using mean pooling over the sequence instead of just the CLS token. Decision: Update model.py to support unfreezing layers and mean pooling. Update train.py to use different learning rates for the ESM layers and the regressor, add a learning rate scheduler, and train for 5 epochs. Submit for evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: The 35M model with 2 unfreezed layers achieved spearman_rho 0.5948. Analysis: To improve performance and generalization to >2 mutations, we can use a larger pre-trained model (ESM-2 150M) and an AttentionPooling layer to allow the model to focus on the mutated region (positions 561-588) rather than simple mean pooling. We will unfreeze 1 layer of the 150M model to prevent overfitting. We will also use mixed precision training (AMP) to speed up training and reduce memory usage. Decision: Update model.py to use ESM-2 150M and AttentionPooling. Update train.py and predict.py to use AMP. Train for 4 epochs and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: The previous run failed with a RuntimeError because `-1e9` cannot be converted to `float16` without overflow during AMP training. Analysis: We need to use a smaller negative value like `-1e4` for the attention mask fill, which is safe for `float16`. I will also reduce the batch size to 16 to prevent potential OOM errors with the larger 150M model. Decision: Update `model.py` to use `-1e4` for masking. Update `train.py` and `predict.py` to use batch size 16. Run training for 4 epochs and submit for evaluation.

[eval_harness] Result: {'spearman_rho': 0.6951, 'pearson_r': 0.7033, 'mse': 4.9149, 'mae': 1.6622}

trajectory.description: Observation: The 150M model with AttentionPooling overfit to the training set (val spearman 0.7886, test spearman 0.4299). The 35M model with MeanPooling generalized better (test spearman 0.5948). Analysis: AttentionPooling and the larger 150M model likely memorized the specific <=2 mutation combinations in the training set, failing to generalize to >2 mutations. To improve generalization, we should revert to the 35M model and use a 1D CNN over the sequence before mean pooling. The CNN can learn local interaction motifs between nearby mutations, which should generalize better to higher-order combinatorial mutants than simple mean pooling or attention. Decision: Update `model.py` to use ESM-2 35M, unfreeze 2 layers, and add a 2-layer 1D CNN before mean pooling. Update `train.py` to train for 6 epochs with batch size 32. Submit for evaluation.

[eval_harness] Result: {'spearman_rho': 0.5958, 'pearson_r': 0.5401, 'mse': 8.6952, 'mae': 2.1669}

trajectory.description: Observation: The 35M model with a 1D CNN and mean pooling achieved a new best spearman_rho of 0.6951. The CNN successfully learned local interaction motifs that generalize well to higher-order mutants. Analysis: We can likely improve this further by increasing the capacity of the CNN (3 layers, kernel size 7) to capture wider motifs, unfreezing one more layer of the ESM model (3 layers total) for better representations, and concatenating max pooling with mean pooling to capture both the average and the strongest motif signals. We will also train for 8 epochs since the model was still improving at epoch 6. Decision: Update model.py with the deeper CNN, max+mean pooling, and unfreeze 3 layers. Update train.py to train for 8 epochs. Submit for evaluation.

[eval_harness] Result: {'spearman_rho': 0.6714, 'pearson_r': 0.6649, 'mse': 6.4717, 'mae': 1.9576}

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 6 overfit significantly (val spearman 0.8799, test spearman 0.5958) compared to Iteration 5 (test spearman 0.6951). The added complexity (3 CNN layers, max pooling, 3 unfreezed ESM layers) hurt generalization to >2 mutations. Analysis: We need to revert to the simpler architecture from Iteration 5 (2 unfreezed layers, 2 CNN layers, mean pooling) which generalized better. To improve upon it, we can increase the CNN kernel size from 7 to 9 to capture slightly wider motifs, increase weight decay to 0.05 for better regularization, and use OneCycleLR for a better learning rate schedule. Decision: Update model.py to revert to 2 unfreezed layers, 2 CNN layers with kernel size 9, and mean pooling. Update train.py to use OneCycleLR and higher weight decay. Train for 6 epochs and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 7 (kernel size 9, weight decay 0.05, OneCycleLR) performed worse (0.6714) than Iteration 5 (0.6951). Analysis: The changes in Iteration 7 likely caused underfitting or suboptimal convergence. We should revert to the core architecture of Iteration 5 (kernel size 7, standard AdamW with CosineAnnealingLR) but add slightly more dropout (0.2 in CNN, 0.3 in regressor) to prevent overfitting to the <=2 mutation training set, and train for 7 epochs to ensure full convergence. Decision: Update model.py and train.py to reflect these changes, run training, and submit for evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 8 achieved a new best test spearman of 0.7175. The architecture with 2 CNN layers (kernel 7) and mean pooling is very effective. Analysis: The mutated region in the AAV capsid is 28 amino acids long (positions 561-588). Currently, the CNN has a receptive field of 19 (kernel 7, dilation 2). If we increase the dilation of the second CNN layer to 4, the receptive field becomes 31 (7 + 6*4), which perfectly covers the entire mutated region. This allows the CNN to capture complex interactions between any mutations in that region. I will also slightly lower the ESM learning rate to 5e-5 to regularize the fine-tuning and train for 10 epochs. Decision: Update model.py to use dilation=4 in the second CNN layer. Update train.py to use lr=5e-5 for ESM and train for 10 epochs. Submit for evaluation.

[eval_harness] Result: {'spearman_rho': 0.7129, 'pearson_r': 0.7214, 'mse': 4.2146, 'mae': 1.539}

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 9 with a larger receptive field (dilation=4) overfit and performed worse on the test set (0.6979) compared to Iteration 8 (0.7175). Analysis: A smaller receptive field (kernel 7, dilation 1) forces the CNN to learn local motifs rather than memorizing the entire mutated region. Furthermore, to extrapolate to >2 mutations, an additive model is theoretically optimal. If we replace the MLP regressor with a simple Linear layer, and use Mean Pooling (which acts like Sum Pooling since lengths are constant), the model will simply sum the contributions of local motifs detected by the CNN. This perfectly models an additive fitness landscape and should drastically improve extrapolation to higher-order mutants. Decision: Revert to kernel 7, dilation 1. Increase CNN channels to 512 for more motif capacity. Replace the MLP regressor with a single Linear layer. Train for 8 epochs.

[eval_harness] Result: {'spearman_rho': 0.7296, 'pearson_r': 0.73, 'mse': 4.9282, 'mae': 1.6763}

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 10 with a single Linear layer performed slightly worse (0.7129) than Iteration 8 with an MLP (0.7175). Analysis: The MLP regressor captures some useful non-linearities. Furthermore, we previously found that the 150M model overfit when using AttentionPooling (Iteration 4), but the CNN + MeanPooling architecture provides a strong inductive bias for additive extrapolation. Combining the larger 150M model (for better representations) with the CNN + MeanPooling architecture (for better extrapolation) could yield the best results. Decision: Update model.py to use ESM-2 150M, unfreeze 1 layer, use a 2-layer CNN (kernel 7, 256 channels), and an MLP regressor. Train for 6 epochs with batch size 32. Submit for evaluation.
